description_long: This is a rounded, ancient-style ceramic vase with a single handle and a narrow neck, reminiscent of Greek black-figure pottery. The body is adorned with intricate black-figure illustrations depicting human and mythological figures, including a centaur and warriors, set against a natural terracotta background. The shoulder of the vase features a decorative band of repeated leaf or petal motifs encircling the neck, while the base and rim are painted black. The handle is robust and curves gracefully from the neck to the shoulder, providing both aesthetic balance and functional grip. The surface appears well-preserved, with clear, detailed artwork and minimal visible wear. This vase would be a striking decorative piece in a refined urban setting, such as a museum or gallery.
description: A rounded, terracotta vase with black-figure Greek-style illustrations, a single handle, and decorative motifs around the neck. The surface is well-preserved and features mythological and human figures.
description_view_0: The front view displays the main illustrated scene with a centaur and a warrior, as well as the decorative band around the neck.
description_view_1: This view highlights the back of the handle and a less-decorated side, with part of a standing figure visible.
description_view_2: The side view shows a single standing figure and the curvature of the handle, with less of the main scene visible.
description_view_3: This view presents another angle of the illustrated scene, focusing on the centaur and a second warrior, with the handle partially visible.
attribute_pattern: mythological scenes with leaf motif band
